From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Chiang Dao District](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Dao_District&redirect=no "Chiang Dao District"))

District in Chiang Mai, Thailand

**Chiang Dao** ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): เชียงดาว, pronounced [[t͡ɕʰīa̯ŋ dāːw]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai"); [Northern Thai](/wiki/Northern_Thai_language "Northern Thai language"): ᨩ᩠ᨿᨦᨯᩣ᩠ᩅ, pronounced [[t͡ɕīa̯ŋ dāːw]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Northern_Thai "Help:IPA/Northern Thai")) is a district (*[amphoe](/wiki/Amphoe "Amphoe")*) of [Chiang Mai province](/wiki/Chiang_Mai_province "Chiang Mai province") in northern [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"). It is nicknamed "little Tuscany" and several wines are produced in the area.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Dao_district&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

Neighboring districts are (from the northeast clockwise) [Fang](/wiki/Fang_district "Fang district"), [Chai Prakan](/wiki/Chai_Prakan_district "Chai Prakan district"), [Phrao](/wiki/Phrao_district "Phrao district"), and [Mae Taeng](/wiki/Mae_Taeng_district "Mae Taeng district") of Chiang Mai Province; [Pai](/wiki/Pai_district "Pai district") of [Mae Hong Son province](/wiki/Mae_Hong_Son_province "Mae Hong Son province"); and [Wiang Haeng](/wiki/Wiang_Haeng_district "Wiang Haeng district") of Chiang Mai. To the north is the [Shan State](/wiki/Shan_State "Shan State") of [Myanmar](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar").

Chiang Dao is the only district in Thailand that has all twelve [hill tribes](/wiki/Hill_tribe_(Thailand) "Hill tribe (Thailand)") in residence.

The [Chiang Dao Wildlife Sanctuary](/wiki/Chiang_Dao_Wildlife_Sanctuary "Chiang Dao Wildlife Sanctuary"), with over 300 species of birds, surrounds the mountain of [Doi Chiang Dao](/wiki/Doi_Chiang_Dao "Doi Chiang Dao").[[1]](#cite_note-1) [Pha Daeng National Park](/wiki/Pha_Daeng_National_Park "Pha Daeng National Park") is another nature reserve located within the district.

The [Ping River](/wiki/Ping_River "Ping River"), one of the main tributaries to the [Chao Phraya River](/wiki/Chao_Phraya_River "Chao Phraya River"), originates at Doi Thuai, in the mountains of the [Daen Lao Range](/wiki/Daen_Lao_Range "Daen Lao Range") in Chiang Dao District. The [Taeng River](/wiki/Taeng_River "Taeng River"), a river that has its source in the mountains of the Daen Lao Range in neighbouring Wiang Haeng District, also flows through Chiang Dao District on its way to its confluence with the [Ping River](/wiki/Ping_River "Ping River") in Mae Taeng District.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Dao_district&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

Chiang Dao was a smaller city state (*[mueang](/wiki/Mueang "Mueang")*) in the northern Thai [Lan Na](/wiki/Lan_Na "Lan Na") Kingdom. As part of the *Thesaphiban* administrative reforms at the end of the 19th century it was made a district of Chiang Mai Province.

Originally a minor district (*[king amphoe](/wiki/King_amphoe "King amphoe")*), it was upgraded to a full district in 1908.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chiang_Dao_district&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Administration")]

The district is divided into seven sub-districts (*[tambon](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon")*), which are further subdivided into 83 villages (*[muban](/wiki/Muban "Muban")*). Both Chiang Dao and Mueang Ngai are sub-district municipalities (*[thesaban tambon](/wiki/Thesaban_tambon "Thesaban tambon")*), which cover parts of the same-named *tambon*s. The sub-district municipality Phra That Pu Kam (พระธาตุปู่ก่ำ) covers the remaining parts of sub-district Mueang Ngai. Mueang Na, Ping Khong, and Thung Khao Phuang are sub-district municipalities which cover the whole of the same-named sub-districts. There are a further three [tambon administrative organizations](/wiki/Tambon_administrative_organization "Tambon administrative organization") (TAO): Mae Na and Mueang Khong covering the same-named sub-districts, and Chiang Dao the non-municipal parts of the sub-district.
